title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power developer board with a Coral NPU accelerator for running Gemma and other on-device AI models. The board ships with peripherals including a screen, camera, and microphones, enabling edge AI applications like live translation, natural language control, and multimodal inference entirely on the device.
writeup: The video covers Google's Coral Board, a low-power developer board with a Coral NPU accelerator built for running Gemma and other AI models on-device. It demonstrates how the board ships with peripherals including a screen, camera, and microphones, supporting edge applications such as live translation, natural language control, and multimodal inference entirely local to the device. The takeaway is that local inference on dedicated hardware enables these workloads without offloading to the cloud.
